name: ml-systems-deconstruction-ontology
description: Operational ontology for residential deconstruction — task decomposition, material taxonomy, crew modeling, and equipment mapping. Bridges humanoid-ontology task primitives to field deconstruction protocols.

humanoidOntology.relationship: This ontology expands the humanoid-ontology DCN layer with operational detail from field research. Task primitives are shared vocabulary — same atom IDs, extended with decon-specific primitives.

fieldExperience.note: ML1 construction experience informs deconstruction sequencing — reverse order preserves structural logic

stats.payStructure: Day N = $N (net), 1-year review gate, payroll engine with RI/federal tax tables

shiftModel.structure: 90 min sustained labor + 45 min problem-solving = 2.25 hrs per shift
shiftModel.philosophy: The 45 is not a break — it's the other half of the job. Labor is the body, the 45 is the brain. Both compensated.
shiftModel.phaseMapping: 12 shifts mapped to PRE → Soft Strip → ROOF → WALLS → FLOORS → FOUNDATION → POST across 2 days

payroll.model: Day N = $N take-home. Employer absorbs all taxes. Daily deposits to workers, weekly/monthly tax remittance per RI/federal compliance.
payroll.reviewGate: Day 365 performance review gates continuation to Year 2 (Day 366+)

futureMerge.shiftFit: Each task code has estimatedMinutes — shiftFit (how many repetitions fit in one 90-min labor block) will be calculated from field data.
futureMerge.robotParams: At 500 verified video clips, task primitives export to robot-params for ML1 scorer integration.

author: ML Systems LLC
